title: paspalum cangarum
intro: Paspalum cangarum, also known as the rice grass, is a type of tropical grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Paspalum cangarum has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and soothing digestive issues. Paspalum cangarum is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be used to make a medicinal oil, which is applied topically to aid in wound healing and skin rejuvenation.
